title: Python Control Flow
description: Practice conditionals, loops, and control flow logic in Python

questions.question: Which keyword starts an if statement in Python?
questions.explanation: Python uses if to begin a conditional statement.

questions.question: What keyword is used for an alternative branch after if?
questions.explanation: else runs when the if condition is false.

questions.question: Which loop is commonly used to iterate over a sequence?
questions.explanation: for loops are used to iterate over items in a sequence.

questions.question: What does range(3) generate?
questions.explanation: range(3) produces values from 0 up to, but not including, 3.

questions.question: Which keyword skips the rest of the current loop iteration?
questions.explanation: continue skips to the next iteration of the loop.

questions.question: Which keyword exits a loop immediately?
questions.explanation: break terminates the loop right away.

questions.question: What symbol is used for comparison in Python?
What is the result of 5 > 2?
questions.explanation: 5 is greater than 2, so the expression is True.

questions.question: Which keyword can be used when a block is required syntactically but no action is needed?
questions.explanation: pass is a no-op statement used when Python requires a statement.

questions.question: What does the else clause on a for loop do?
options: Runs only if the loop completes normally
Runs only if break is used
questions.explanation: The else block runs if the loop finishes without encountering break.
